templates.name: Conversation
templates.description: For chat-based agents and assistants. Tracks user preferences, conversation patterns, and builds a profile over time.

manifest.description: General-purpose conversation memory for chat agents and assistants

bank.reflect_mission: You are a memory assistant for a conversational AI. Help recall user preferences, past interactions, and important context. Focus on what the user cares about and how they like to interact.
bank.retain_mission: Extract user preferences, stated facts about themselves, requests they've made, topics they care about, and any commitments or follow-ups. Ignore small talk and filler.
bank.observations_mission: Track stable user preferences, communication style, recurring topics, and how the user's needs evolve over time.

mental_models.name: User Profile
mental_models.source_query: What do we know about this user? What are their preferences, background, and how do they like to interact?

mental_models.name: Open Threads
mental_models.source_query: What topics, tasks, or follow-ups are still open or unresolved from past conversations?

directives.name: Respect stated preferences
directives.content: Always respect explicitly stated user preferences (e.g. communication style, format, topics to avoid). These take priority over inferred patterns.

directives.name: No hallucinated history
directives.content: Never fabricate or assume past interactions that aren't in memory. If unsure whether something was discussed before, say so.

templates.name: Coding Agent
templates.description: For coding assistants. Remembers project architecture, technical decisions, coding patterns, and user preferences across sessions. High literalism for precise technical recall.

manifest.description: Memory for coding agents — tracks technical decisions, project context, and developer preferences

bank.reflect_mission: You are a memory assistant for a coding agent. Help recall technical decisions, project architecture, coding conventions, and the developer's tool and library preferences. Be precise and factual.
bank.retain_mission: Extract technical decisions and their rationale, architectural choices, coding patterns and conventions, project structure facts, library/tool preferences, and recurring issues. Ignore transient debugging output and boilerplate.
bank.observations_mission: Track stable project facts: tech stack, team conventions, architecture patterns, and how the codebase evolves over time.

mental_models.name: Project Context
mental_models.source_query: What is the project's tech stack, architecture, and key conventions? What are the main components and how do they fit together?

mental_models.name: Developer Preferences
mental_models.source_query: What are the developer's preferences for tools, libraries, coding style, and workflow? How do they like code to be written and reviewed?

directives.name: Cite file paths
directives.content: When referencing code or technical decisions, always include the relevant file path. Never give vague references like 'the main file' or 'the config'.

directives.name: Decisions need rationale
directives.content: When recalling a technical decision, always include the rationale (why it was chosen, what alternatives were considered). A decision without context is not useful.
